steps.title: Use corresponding angles
steps.content: If two lines cut by a transversal have equal corresponding angles, then the two lines are parallel. This is the converse of the corresponding angles relationship.
steps.formula: $$w^{\circ} \text{ at line } j \text{ corresponds to } 40^{\circ} \text{ at line } k$$

steps.title: Match the marked angle
steps.content: The angle labeled $w$ is in the same relative position as the marked $40$ degree angle on the other line. If $w=40$, the corresponding angles are equal, so lines $j$ and $k$ are parallel. The answer is A.
